content: For a floating body, buoyant force acts at the

options.content: center of gravity of the body
centroid  of the fluid vertically below the body

content: For a completely submerged body with center of gravity $$'G'$$ and center of buoyancy $$'B',$$ the condition of stability will be

options.content: Independent of the locations of $$G$$ and $$B$$

content: For a completely submerged body with centre of gravity $$‘G’$$ and centre of buoyancy $$‘B’,$$ the condition of stability will be

options.content: Independent of the locations of $$G$$ and $$B$$

content:
For the stability of a floating body, under the influence of gravity alone, which of
the following is TRUE?

options.content: Metacentre should be below centre of gravity
Metacentre should be above centre of gravity
Metacentre and centre of gravity must lie on the same horizontal line
Metacentre and centre of gravity must lie on the same vertical line.

content: A cylindrical body of cross - sectional area A, height $$H$$ and density $${\rho _s},$$ is immersed to a depth $$h$$ in a liquid of density $$\rho ,$$ and tied to the bottom with a string. The tension in the string is
A static fluid can have

options.content: Negative normal stress and zero shear stress
Positive normal stress and zero shear stress
Zero normal stress and non-zero shear stress

content: A mercury manometer is used to measure the static pressure at a point in a water pipe as shown in Fig. The level difference of mercury in the two limbs is $$10$$ $$mm.$$ The gauge pressure at that point is
Bodies in floatation to be in stable equilibrium the necessary and sufficient condition is that the center of gravity is located below the _____________

Shown below are three cylindrical gates which restrain water in $$2$$-$$D$$ channel. Which gate experiences the maximum vertical component, the minimum vertical component and the maximum horizontal component of the hydrostatic force?
answer:
Maximum vertical component is experienced by (A)
Minimum vertical component is experienced by (C)
Maximum Horizontal component is experienced by (C)

content:
The figure shows two fluids held by a hinged gate. The atmospheric pressure is Pa = 100 kPa. The moment per unit width about the base of the hinge is ____________ kNm/m. (Rounded off to one decimal place)

Take the acceleration due to gravity to be g = 9.8 m/s2.
explanation:
$\begin{aligned} & P_1=\frac{(1000 \times 9.81 \times 1)}{1000} \\\\ & P_1=9.81 \mathrm{KPa} \\\\ & P_2=P_1+\rho_2 \mathrm{gh}_2=9.81+\frac{2000 \times 9.81 \times 2}{2} \\\\ & P_2=49.05 \mathrm{KPa}\end{aligned}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \bar{x}=\left(\frac{P_2+2 P_1}{P_1+P_2}\right) \times \frac{2}{3}=\frac{7}{9} \\\\ & F_1=\frac{1}{2} \times P_1 \times 1 \times 1 \\\\ & F_2=\left(P_1+P_2\right) \times \frac{2}{2}=58.86 \mathrm{KN} \\\\ & M_0=F_1 \times \frac{7}{3}+F_2 \times \bar{x} \\\\ & M_0=57.225 \mathrm{KN}-\mathrm{m} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 57.225 .

content:
A uniform wooden rod (specific gravity = 0.6, diameter = 4 cm and length = 8 m) is immersed in the water and is hinged without friction at point A on the waterline as shown in the figure. A solid spherical ball made of lead (specific gravity = 11.4) is attached to the free end of the rod to keep the assembly in static equilibrium inside the water. For simplicity, assume that the radius of the ball is much smaller than the length of the rod.

Assume density of water = 103 kg/m3 and π = 3.14.

Radius of the ball is _______ cm (round off to 2 decimal places).
explanation:
Given :

Specific gravity of the wooden rod $ S_1 = 0.6 $

Diameter of the rod $ D_1 = 4 \text{ cm} \Rightarrow \mathrm{r}_1 = 2 \text{ cm} $

Length of the rod $ l = 8 \text{ m} $

Specific gravity of the spherical ball $ S_2 = 11.4 $

Weight of the rod $ W_1 = \rho_1 g \pi r_1^2 l $

Weight of the spherical ball $ W_2 = \rho_2 g \frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3 $

Where :

$ r_1 $ = radius of the rod

$ r_2 $ = radius of the sphere

Buoyant force :

$ F_B = \rho_w g \left( \pi r_1^2 l + \frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3 \right) $

For equilibrium :

$ \sum M_A = 0 $

The moments about point A must balance :

$ W_1 \cdot x + W_2 \cdot 2x = F_1 \cdot x + F_B \cdot 2x $

Simplifying, we get :

$ \rho_1 g \pi r_1^2 l + 2 \rho_2 g \frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3 = \rho_w g \pi r_1^2 l + \frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3 \cdot 2 $

Substituting the known specific gravities and densities :

$ S_1 \pi l r_1^2 + 2 S_2 g \frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3 = \pi r_1^2 l + \frac{4}{3} \pi r_2^3 \cdot 2 $

Rearranging the terms to solve for $ r_2 $ :

$ \left(2 S_2 - 2\right) \frac{4}{3} r_2^3 = \left(1 - S_1\right) r_1^2 l $

Substituting the values :

$ (2 \times 11.2 - 2) \frac{4}{3} r_2^3 = (1 - 0.6) \times 8 \times (0.02)^2 $

$ r_2^3 = 0.03610 \text{ m} $

$ r_2 = 3.61 \text{ cm} $

Hence, the radius of the ball is $ 3.61 \text{ cm} $.

content: For the stability of a floating body the

options.content: centre of buoyancy must coincide with the centre of gravity
centre of buoyancy must be above the centre of gravity
centre of gravity must be above the centre of buoyancy
metacentre must be above the centre of gravity
